data.task:
AUDIT ONLY — produce an evidence-backed findings document; change NO behavior and edit NO daemon code. Goal: enumerate every piece of daemon global/singleton state that assumes a single active run, so the next atoms can scope the right ones per-workspace.

Deliverable: write a new markdown audit at docs/concurrency/multi-workspace-audit.md (create the dir). Structure it as a table/list where every entry has: (a) the symbol/site with file:line evidence, (b) what it currently assumes, (c) classification — one of {LEGITIMATELY GLOBAL (e.g. engine self-reload that must wait on any run in flight), MUST BE PER-WORKSPACE (blocks cross-workspace launch and should be scoped by workspaceId), or ALREADY PER-RUN/PER-WORKSPACE SAFE}, and (d) a one-line proposed action for the per-workspace ones.

Mandatory coverage (grep the live tree; do not trust this list as complete — find the full site set yourself):
- ctx.inFlight usage: enumerate EVERY read/write and every `inFlight.size`/`inFlight.has(...)` check. For each `inFlight.size > 0` global check (e.g. the daemon self-reload refusal) decide global vs per-workspace.
- daemonReload / single pending-reload slot.
- the UI-bundle rebuild path.
- dashboard/status feed and any 'current run' / single-active-run assumption in the status projection.
- run-driver (runRun) and lifecycle entry points: any module-level mutable singleton shared across runs.
- CONFIRM (with file:line) whether stopControllers, the WAL/SQLite store, the atomic run-counter, and cmux surface tracking are already per-run/per-workspace keyed. Each gets an explicit ALREADY-SAFE verdict with the keying evidence, or is flagged.

Constraints: read-only audit — write ONLY docs/concurrency/multi-workspace-audit.md. Cite real file:line from this repo (no invented paths). Do not reclassify ADR-0045's isolation boundary: the hard boundary is the workspace/root; do not propose any intra-root path lane as a hardness boundary. Acceptance: the doc enumerates the complete inFlight site set with file:line, classifies each suspect, and gives explicit per-run/per-workspace-safe verdicts (with evidence) for stopControllers, WAL/SQLite store, run-counter, and cmux tracking.

data.reason: Audit doc is accurate and evidence-backed; spot-checked citations (context.ts:125, launcher 407/429/841/2064/2251/2296, sqlite-store:148) match exactly, and the two MUST-BE-PER-WORKSPACE findings (requestOzRepair/requestOzAction guard workspace-targeted ops with global inFlight.size>0) are confirmed; docs-only, scope respected.

data.task:
Scope the two workspace-targeted Oz guards from global to per-workspace so a run on workspace A does not block an Oz repair/action on workspace B. This implements Scope item 2 for the two MUST-BE-PER-WORKSPACE sites the audit found (docs/concurrency/multi-workspace-audit.md).

CHANGE (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts):
1. `requestOzRepair` (currently `if (ctx.inFlight.size > 0)` at ~line 2251): replace the GLOBAL check with a PER-WORKSPACE check on the request's target — `if (ctx.inFlight.has(input.workspaceId))`. Update the 409 error message to say the TARGET WORKSPACE has a run in flight (it would share that workspace's working tree), not 'a run'. Keep the same 409 status and return shape.
2. `requestOzAction` (currently `if (ctx.inFlight.size > 0)` at ~line 2296): same change — guard on `ctx.inFlight.has(input.workspaceId)`, update the 409 message symmetrically.

These are symmetric counterparts; fix BOTH. Both functions already take `input.workspaceId`, and Oz repair/action commit only into that workspace's repo, so a run in a DIFFERENT workspace cannot be orphaned by them — scoping to workspaceId is the correct guard and matches the launch-path guard (`inFlight.has(workspaceId)` at launcher.ts:841).

DO NOT touch the genuinely-global `ctx.inFlight.size` guards — daemon reload drain (launcher.ts:407,429), manual daemon restart (launcher.ts:2064), stale self-restart (launcher.ts:950): those bounce the one daemon process and must stay global. Do not introduce any intra-root path/write-scope lane as a hardness boundary (ADR-0045); the hard boundary stays the workspace/root. Do not add a new 'touches install-wide files' branch — that is out of scope for this atom.

TESTS: grep the daemon test suite for any test asserting the old global refusal of requestOzRepair/requestOzAction (the 'a run is in flight' message or size-based refusal) and update it. ADD coverage proving the new behavior: (a) an Oz repair AND an Oz action on workspace B are ALLOWED (not 409) while a DIFFERENT workspace A has an in-flight run; (b) each is still refused with 409 when the SAME target workspace has an in-flight run. Use the existing daemon test harness/fixtures for inFlight setup.

Write scope: packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts and the relevant daemon test file(s). Acceptance: typecheck passes and the affected daemon test suite is green (run it and report the command + result); cross-workspace Oz repair/action no longer blocked by another workspace's run; same-workspace still blocked. Must not break: launch-path guard, reload/restart global guards, existing error-shape/status contracts.

data.reason: Both Oz guards now scope to inFlight.has(input.workspaceId) with symmetric messages; global restart/reload guards untouched; daemon tsc clean and oz-repair/oz-action/oz-chat suites green (84 passed), including new cross-workspace allow + same-workspace refuse cases.

data.task:
Implement Scope item 4 of the multi-workspace concurrency priority: a global concurrency ceiling on simultaneous runs across workspaces, plus surfacing the active-run count. The launch path is already per-workspace (inFlight.has(workspaceId) at launcher.ts:841); this adds a GLOBAL cap on total in-flight runs so a founder cannot accidentally fan out beyond machine/budget limits (N concurrent runs ≈ 3N agent processes + N× model spend).

FIRST, confirm current state: grep packages/daemon and packages/core to confirm there is NO existing global concurrency ceiling, and read the existing active-run projection (packages/daemon/src/oz-awareness.ts:~32 computes `activeRuns` by status) so you REUSE it rather than building a second active-run counter (one-owner rule).

CHANGE 1 — ceiling enforcement (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts launch path):
- Add a global ceiling check in the deterministic launch path, evaluated when reserving a NEW workspace run (before/at the pending reservation `inFlight.set(workspaceId,'pending')` ~launcher.ts:845). If accepting this launch would make `inFlight.size` exceed the ceiling, refuse with a clear 409 error naming the active count and the ceiling (e.g. `refusing to launch: N runs already in flight (ceiling M) — wait for one to finish or raise the ceiling`). 
- IMPORTANT semantics: a launch for a workspace that is ALREADY in flight is handled by the existing per-workspace guard and must keep its current behavior — do not double-count or change it. Resuming/promoting a run already counted in inFlight must not be blocked by the ceiling. The ceiling only gates ADMITTING a NEW concurrent workspace beyond M.
- Make the ceiling a single configurable value with a sensible default of 3. Put it on the existing daemon settings/config surface (follow the same pattern as the retention keep-count setting — find it and mirror it); do NOT invent a parallel config file. Read the configured value (fall back to default 3) at the guard.
- Do NOT touch the genuinely-global restart/reload guards (launcher.ts:407,429,2064,950) and do NOT change the Oz repair/action per-workspace guards just landed.

CHANGE 2 — surface active-run count across workspaces:
- Expose the global active-run count and the configured ceiling where the founder can see it. Reuse the existing `activeRuns` computation in oz-awareness rather than adding a new projection. Ensure at least one founder-visible surface (the oz-awareness/status output and/or the dashboard status feed) reports `{ activeRuns: <count>, ceiling: <M> }` across all workspaces. Keep the dashboard's per-workspace run list unchanged; this is an additional global counter, not a replacement.

TESTS (packages/daemon/tests/**): 
- Ceiling enforced: with the ceiling at M and M distinct workspaces in flight, a launch on an (M+1)th NEW workspace is refused 409 with the count/ceiling message; with fewer than M in flight, a new-workspace launch is admitted.
- A launch/resume on a workspace already in flight is NOT additionally blocked by the ceiling (existing per-workspace semantics preserved).
- Config: a configured ceiling overrides the default; default is 3 when unset.
- The active-run-count surface reports the correct global count and ceiling.

Write scope: packages/daemon/src/** (launcher.ts, oz-awareness/routes/config as needed), packages/daemon/tests/**, and the config/settings type if it lives in packages/core/src/** . Acceptance: typecheck passes and the affected daemon suites are green (report the command + result). Must not break: existing per-workspace launch guard, Oz repair/action guards, global restart/reload guards, retention config, and the per-workspace dashboard list. Flag in your summary the exact default ceiling value and where the config knob lives so the founder can adjust it.

data.reason: Global ceiling (default 3, configurable via maxConcurrentRuns in settings) enforced at launch after the per-workspace guard so already-in-flight workspaces aren't double-counted; active-run count surfaced by reusing oz-awareness; daemon tsc clean and affected suites green (284 passed) incl. ceiling-enforce/below-ceiling/per-workspace-precedence/settings-sanitization cases.

data.task:
Implement Scope item 3 of the multi-workspace concurrency priority: a deterministic load-harness/test that drives TWO independent runs on TWO different workspaces concurrently in one daemon and proves they progress simultaneously with NO cross-contamination. This is the priority's keystone acceptance test and its regression guard.

Reuse the existing daemon test machinery in packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts (createExternalWorkspace, writeWorkspacesRegistry, controlledDirectiveIO, fakeGit, validFounderCloseout, headless drivers, startServer/call) rather than building new fixtures — one-owner rule. Add the harness as a focused test (in mutations.test.ts or a new packages/daemon/tests/concurrency.test.ts that imports the same helpers, whichever is cleaner).

The harness MUST assert ALL of these (each maps to an Acceptance bullet):
1. SIMULTANEITY (not just sequential success): launch a run on workspace A and a run on workspace B and prove BOTH are in flight at the same time — e.g. hold both runs mid-flight via controlledDirectiveIO and assert ctx.inFlight.size === 2 with both workspaceIds present BEFORE releasing either. A run active on A must not have blocked launching B (the launch returns 202, not 409).
2. INDEPENDENT PROGRESS: release both and drive each to a terminal/wrap state; assert each run reached its own completion independently (both have run records with the expected terminal status).
3. NO COMMIT CROSS-CONTAMINATION: each workspace's commits are attributed to its own run/working tree only — assert run A's committed files/SHAs landed in workspace A and run B's in workspace B, with no bleed across repos.
4. RUN-RECORD / LEDGER ISOLATION: store.listRuns({workspaceId:'A'}) returns only A's run and listRuns({workspaceId:'B'}) only B's; events/sessions are scoped by the correct runId; no run record carries the other workspace's id.
5. NUMBERING RACE-FREE: the global technical run ids are unique and monotonic across the two concurrent createRun calls (no duplicate/collision), and the portable per-root DISPLAY counters (cocoder/counters.json under each primary root) increment independently and correctly — neither root's display number leaks into the other. If feasible, exercise concurrent createRun closely enough to stress the atomic counter / file lock.
6. PANE/SURFACE ISOLATION: each run's cmux/session surface refs are keyed to its own run/workspace; assert no shared surface ref between the two runs.

Determinism: do NOT write a timing/sleep-flaky test. Use controlledDirectiveIO (or equivalent gating already in the harness) to deterministically interleave and hold the two runs so the size===2 overlap is guaranteed, not probabilistic.

If the harness uncovers a REAL isolation/race bug (cross-contamination, counter collision, shared surface), FIX THE ROOT CAUSE in packages/core/src/** or packages/daemon/src/** — do not weaken the assertion or the harness to make it pass, and do not introduce an intra-root path lane as a boundary (ADR-0045: the hard boundary is the workspace/root). If everything already isolates correctly (expected — this priority is validation+hardening of a model that already permits one run per workspace), the deliverable is the passing harness alone.

Write scope: packages/daemon/tests/** (primary), and packages/core/src/** or packages/daemon/src/** ONLY if a real bug must be fixed at root cause. Acceptance: typecheck passes and the new harness plus the existing daemon suite are green (report the exact command + result, and the run ids/inFlight.size observed proving simultaneity). Report whether any root-cause fix was needed or the capability already held.

data.reason: Concurrency harness proves all six dimensions: deterministic inFlight.size===2 simultaneity (both launches 202, run ids [1,2]), independent completion, per-workspace listRuns/events/sessions isolation, commit files/links excluding the other run, per-root counters.json + run.json display numbers, and disjoint surface refs; test-only (no root-cause bug — capability already held), daemon tsc clean, mutations suite green (160) and the harness stable across 3 isolated reruns.

data.task:
Documentation atom: make the now-shipped multi-workspace run concurrency current-truth in the permanent docs. Two precise edits; do NOT duplicate the audit (docs/concurrency/multi-workspace-audit.md is the analysis owner — reference it, don't restate it).

EDIT 1 — docs/configuration.md: the live `local/settings.json` keys list (around line 44-47: `pollIntervalMs`, `defaultWorkspaceId`, `ozAutoCompactRuns`, `retention`) is now incomplete. Add `maxConcurrentRuns` to that list with a one-line description: the global ceiling on simultaneously in-flight runs ACROSS workspaces, default 3, values <=0 or invalid fall back to the default (matches packages/daemon/src/settings.ts saneMaxConcurrentRuns + DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENT_RUNS=3). Keep the existing wording/format for the other keys.

EDIT 2 — ARCHITECTURE.md, the 'Multi-workspace concurrency (plain language)' section (~line 363): it currently explains only pane/surface isolation. Add a short, plain-language statement of the run-concurrency model now that it is real: runs in DIFFERENT workspaces execute concurrently (the launch guard and the inFlight map are keyed by workspaceId, so a run on workspace A does not block a launch on workspace B); runs in the SAME workspace remain serialized (shared working tree); genuinely-global operations (daemon self-reload/restart) still wait on all in-flight runs; and total concurrency is bounded by the `maxConcurrentRuns` ceiling (default 3) surfaced as `Active runs: N/M` in Oz status. Note intra-workspace concurrency is out of scope (deferred to v2, ADR-0042 Tier 3) and the hard isolation boundary remains the workspace/root (ADR-0045). Link to docs/concurrency/multi-workspace-audit.md for the per-site evidence. Keep it tight (a short paragraph or a few bullets) — do not restate the audit table.

Write scope: docs/configuration.md and ARCHITECTURE.md only. Acceptance: both edits land as described; grep/run any doc-contract or truth-audit test that pins the configuration.md settings-key list or ARCHITECTURE content (there have been such audits — check for a test asserting the settings keys against settings.ts and run it) and confirm green; report the command + result. Must not break: existing doc-pinning tests, the other documented settings keys, and one-owner (no duplication of the audit doc).

data.reason: configuration.md now lists maxConcurrentRuns (default 3, <=0/invalid fallback) and ARCHITECTURE.md's concurrency section states the now-real bounded cross-workspace model with correct ADR-0042/0045 links and an audit reference (no duplication); doc-link validators drift-resolve-doc-references/drift-read-claims and contract/read-surface suites green (54 passed).
